title: Heavy-Duty 2x4 Sawhorses (Pair)
summary: A pair of plain, non-stacking 2x4 sawhorses built from a T-shaped leg spine, angled leg sets, and diagonal 1x3 cross bracing — genuinely simple shop utility, no nesting feature.
description:
Each leg set here starts as a small T: a short bottom foot piece screwed to a vertical center piece, aligning the foot's center on the center piece so it sits balanced under the leg. From there, attach the angled 2x4 legs to the bottom foot piece 8" up from the bottom, then attach the tops of the same legs to the center piece — build two identical leg sets per sawhorse.

Join the two leg sets with the top 2x4 running the length of the sawhorse plus a bottom support 2x4 underneath, both attached with screws. **The diagonal 1x3 cross pieces go inside the sawhorse last**, screwed in place to keep the whole thing from racking side to side under load — this is what turns two wobbly leg sets into a genuinely heavy-duty stand. Build two sawhorses the same way.

cutList.note: Both ends cut at 15° off square, ends not parallel, longest point measurement.
Both ends cut at 15° off square, parallel, long point to short point measurement.
Both ends cut parallel at 18° off square, long point to short point measurement.

steps.title: Build the leg-set spines
steps.body: Attach a bottom foot piece to a center piece with 2-1/2" screws, aligning the center of the bottom piece with the center piece.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Attach the legs to the bottom foot piece with 2-1/2" screws, 8" up from the bottom of the leg. Attach the tops of the legs to the center piece. Build two leg sets per sawhorse.

steps.title: Join the leg sets
steps.body: Attach the top support rail and bottom support rail to both leg sets with 2-1/2" screws.

steps.title: Add the cross bracing
steps.body: Place the diagonal cross pieces inside the sawhorse and attach with 1-1/2" screws. Repeat all steps to build the second sawhorse.
